name: Chilean Pan Amasado with Avocado
steps: Mix the flour with the butter, a pinch of salt, and enough warm water to form a soft dough; knead briefly and rest 15 minutes.
Shape into a flat round about 3/4 inch thick and cook in a lightly oiled skillet over medium-low heat, 8-10 minutes per side, until golden and cooked through.
Split while warm and spread with the mashed avocado.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, baking powder, and a pinch of salt, then rub in the butter until the mixture looks like coarse crumbs.
Add warm water gradually, mixing until a soft, slightly tacky dough forms. Knead briefly on a floured surface, then cover and rest 15-20 minutes.
Shape the dough into a disc about 3/4 inch thick and prick the top a few times with a fork.
Cook in a lightly oiled heavy skillet over medium-low heat, 8-10 minutes per side, until deeply golden and cooked through in the center - lower the heat if it's browning too fast.
Mash the avocado with the lime juice and a pinch of salt.
Split the warm bread and spread generously with the avocado.

name: Chilean Scrambled Eggs with Tomato and Onion
steps: Saute the onion in the butter until soft.
Add the diced tomatoes and cook 2-3 minutes to reduce slightly.
Pour in the beaten eggs and scramble gently until just set.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion in the butter over medium heat for 4-5 minutes until soft and translucent.
Add the diced tomatoes and black pepper, and cook 3-4 minutes until slightly reduced.
Beat the eggs lightly and pour into the pan, stirring gently and continuously over low heat until just set and creamy - don't overcook.
Stir in the cilantro just before serving.

name: Chilean Mote con Leche
steps: Simmer the rice (or wheat berries if you have them) in water until fully tender, then drain.
Warm the milk with the brown sugar until just simmering.
Spoon the cooked grain into a bowl and pour the warm sweetened milk over it.
steps_detailed: Simmer the rice in water until very tender, 15-18 minutes, then drain well.
Warm the milk with the brown sugar and cinnamon over medium-low heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves and the milk is hot but not boiling.
Spoon the warm grain into a bowl and pour the sweetened milk over the top; let it sit a minute so the grain soaks up some of the milk before eating.

name: Chilean Pastel de Choclo
steps: Brown the ground beef with the onion until cooked through; season and set aside in a small baking dish.
Blend the corn with the milk until mostly smooth but still a little textured, then cook in a pot over medium heat, stirring, until thickened into a soft mash.
Spread the corn mash over the beef and bake at 400F for 20-25 minutes until lightly golden on top.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion in half the butter over medium heat until soft, then add the ground beef, cumin, and black pepper, browning the meat fully, 6-8 minutes.
Spread the beef mixture in a small baking dish and tuck the sliced hard-boiled egg and olives into it.
Blend the corn kernels with the milk until mostly smooth, leaving some texture.
Melt the remaining butter in a pot, add the corn mixture and brown sugar, and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, 8-10 minutes until it thickens into a spreadable mash.
Spread the corn mash evenly over the beef layer, all the way to the edges.
Bake at 400F (200C) for 20-25 minutes until the top is set and lightly golden in spots.

name: Chilean Porotos Granados

ingredients.ingredient: Lima beans, large, mature seeds, cooked, boiled, with salt

steps: Saute the chayote in a splash of oil until starting to soften.
Add the beans, corn, and diced tomatoes with a cup of water, and simmer 15 minutes until the chayote is tender.
Mash a portion of the beans against the side of the pot to thicken the stew slightly.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Lima beans, large, mature seeds, cooked, boiled, with salt

steps_detailed: Saute the onion and garlic in the oil for 4-5 minutes until soft, then add the chayote and cook 5 more minutes until it starts to soften.
Add the beans, corn, diced tomatoes, oregano, and 1.5 cups water. Bring to a simmer.
Cook 15-18 minutes until the chayote is fully tender and the broth has thickened slightly.
Mash about a quarter of the beans against the side of the pot to naturally thicken the stew.

name: Chilean Arroz con Pollo
steps: Brown the chicken thigh in a pot, then remove and set aside.
Saute the carrot and green beans in the same pot, add the rice, and stir to coat.
Return the chicken to the pot, add broth to cover, and simmer covered 20 minutes until the rice is tender.
steps_detailed: Season the chicken with black pepper and brown it in the oil over medium-high heat, 5-6 minutes per side, then remove and set aside.
In the same pot, saute the onion, bell pepper, garlic, carrot, and green beans for 5-6 minutes until starting to soften.
Add the rice and stir for a minute to coat it in the fat and vegetables.
Return the chicken to the pot, pour in the chicken broth, bring to a simmer, then cover and cook over low heat 20-25 minutes until the rice is tender and the chicken is cooked through.
Let it rest covered for 5 minutes before serving.

name: Chilean Ensalada Chilena
steps: Drain the tomatoes well and slice the onion thinly.
Toss together with the cilantro and a drizzle of oil, salting well.
Let sit 10 minutes before serving so the onion softens slightly.
steps_detailed: Drain the diced tomatoes well and slice the onion very thinly.
Rinse the sliced onion briefly under cold water to soften its bite, then pat dry.
Toss the tomatoes and onion with the cilantro, oil, and lime juice, and season with salt.
Let sit at room temperature 10-15 minutes before serving so the onion softens and the flavors meld.

name: Chilean Cazuela de Vacuno

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Simmer the beef in the broth with a cup of water for 30 minutes until tender.
Add the sweet potato and simmer 15 minutes more.
Add the corn and simmer 5 minutes, then season and serve in the broth.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Combine the beef, broth, 1.5 cups water, onion, bay leaf, and oregano in a pot. Bring to a simmer and cook 30-35 minutes until the beef is tender.
Add the sweet potato and carrot, and simmer 15 minutes.
Add the corn and green beans, and simmer 8-10 more minutes until all the vegetables are tender.
Remove the bay leaf, stir in the cilantro, and serve hot in wide bowls with the broth.

name: Chilean Charquican
steps: Boil the sweet potato until tender, then mash roughly.
Brown the beef with the onion, then stir in the mashed sweet potato and corn.
Cook everything together 5 minutes, mashing further so it becomes a thick hash.
steps_detailed: Boil the sweet potato and green beans together until very tender, 12-15 minutes, then drain and mash roughly, leaving some texture.
Saute the onion and garlic in the oil for 4-5 minutes until soft, then add the ground beef and cumin, browning fully.
Stir the mashed sweet potato mixture and corn into the beef, mashing everything together into a thick, rustic hash.
Cook 5-6 more minutes, stirring, until well combined and heated through.
Top with the sliced hard-boiled egg before serving.

name: Chilean Empanadas de Pino
steps: Saute the onion slowly until very soft and sweet, about 10 minutes, then brown the beef with it.
Spoon the beef and onion filling onto the tortillas along with the chopped egg, fold and seal the edges.
Bake at 400F for 15-18 minutes until golden, or pan-fry if you'd rather.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Saute the onion slowly in the oil over low heat for 10-12 minutes until very soft and sweet, almost jam-like.
Add the ground beef, cumin, and black pepper, and cook until the beef is browned, 6-8 minutes. Let the mixture cool.
Make a simple dough: rub the butter into the flour with a pinch of salt, then add warm water gradually until a smooth dough forms. Rest 15 minutes, then roll and cut into rounds.
Spoon the cooled beef filling onto each round along with a bit of chopped egg, a few raisins, and a piece of olive.
Fold the dough over into a half-moon and crimp the edges firmly to seal, or fold into the traditional pointed shape.
Bake at 400F (200C) for 18-22 minutes until golden brown.

name: Chilean Seafood and Sausage Stew
steps: Simmer the pork in water 15 minutes, then add the sweet potato and cabbage and cook 15 more minutes.
Add the shrimp and simmer 4-5 minutes until just cooked through.
Serve everything together in the broth.
steps_detailed: Combine the pork, onion, garlic, bay leaf, and 2 cups water in a pot. Simmer 15-18 minutes until the pork is nearly tender.
Add the sweet potato and cabbage, and continue simmering 15 minutes until tender.
Add the shrimp and simmer gently 4-5 minutes, just until pink and cooked through.
Remove the bay leaf, stir in the cilantro, and serve in bowls with the broth.

name: Chilean Sopaipillas
steps: Mix the flour with the mashed sweet potato (or pumpkin) and a pinch of salt into a firm dough.
Roll thin and cut into small rounds, pricking each a few times with a fork.
Fry in hot oil 2-3 minutes per side until golden and puffed.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, mashed sweet potato, melted butter, and a pinch of salt into a firm, slightly sticky dough. Knead briefly until smooth.
Roll the dough out to about 1/4 inch thick and cut into 3-inch rounds, pricking each all over with a fork to keep them from puffing unevenly.
Heat the oil in a skillet over medium heat until shimmering. Fry the rounds 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through.
Drain on a paper towel and serve warm, plain or with a drizzle of syrup.

name: Chilean Completo Italiano

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Uncured Beef Hot Dogs, Uncured Beef

steps: Warm the hot dog and bun.
Mash the avocado with a pinch of salt.
Layer the hot dog with the avocado, diced tomato, and a line of mayo.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Uncured Beef Hot Dogs, Uncured Beef

steps_detailed: Warm the hot dog through and toast the bun lightly.
Mash the avocado with a pinch of salt until smooth (this is the 'italiano' - green, white, red like the Italian flag).
Place the hot dog in the bun and top generously with the mashed avocado, then the diced tomato.
Finish with a drizzle of the sour cream (standing in for the traditional mayonnaise) and a scattering of cilantro.

name: Chilean Empanadas de Queso
steps: Fill each tortilla with the crumbled queso fresco, fold in half, and press the edges to seal.
Fry in the oil 2 minutes per side until golden and the cheese has melted.
Drain briefly before serving hot.
steps_detailed: Combine the queso fresco and mozzarella in a bowl.
Spoon the cheese mixture onto one half of each tortilla, fold over, and press the edges firmly to seal, crimping with a fork if needed.
Heat the oil in a skillet over medium heat and fry the filled turnovers 2-3 minutes per side until golden and the cheese inside is melted.
Drain on a paper towel and serve immediately while the cheese is still gooey.

name: Chilean Leche Asada
steps: Whisk the milk, egg, sugar, and vanilla together until smooth.
Pour into a small baking dish and set that dish into a larger pan of hot water.
Bake at 350F for 30-35 minutes until just set with a slight wobble in the center.
steps_detailed: Whisk the milk, eggs, sugar, and vanilla together thoroughly until the sugar is dissolved and no streaks of egg remain.
Pour the mixture into a small buttered baking dish.
Set the dish into a larger roasting pan and add hot water to come halfway up the sides of the baking dish (a water bath).
Bake at 350F (175C) for 30-40 minutes, until just set with a gentle wobble in the center - it will firm up more as it cools.
Let cool, then chill at least an hour. Dust with cinnamon before serving.

name: Chilean Mote con Huesillos
steps: Simmer the dried fruit with the brown sugar and 1.5 cups water for 15 minutes until softened, forming a light syrup.
Cook the rice (or wheat berries) separately in water until tender, then drain.
Combine the cooked grain with the fruit and its syrup, chilling before serving.
steps_detailed: Simmer the dried fruit with the brown sugar, cinnamon, and 2 cups water for 15-20 minutes until the fruit is soft and plump and the liquid has become a light syrup.
Meanwhile, cook the rice in a separate pot of water until very tender, 15-18 minutes, then drain.
Combine the cooked grain with the stewed fruit and its syrup in a pitcher or bowl.
Chill well before serving in tall glasses, spooning some of the fruit and grain into each one.

name: Chilean Alfajores
steps: Cream the butter and sugar, then work in the flour to form a soft dough. Rest 15 minutes.
Roll thin, cut into small rounds, and bake at 350F for 8-10 minutes until just set (not browned).
Sandwich pairs of cookies together with the dulce de leche.
steps_detailed: Cream the butter and sugar together until light, then mix in the egg yolk.
Sift together the flour and cornstarch and fold into the butter mixture until a soft, slightly crumbly dough forms. Wrap and chill 20 minutes.
Roll the dough out to about 1/4 inch thick and cut into small rounds (a shot glass works well).
Bake at 325F (165C) for 8-10 minutes - they should stay pale, not browned, since the cornstarch keeps them tender.
Let cool completely, then sandwich pairs together with a generous layer of dulce de leche in the middle.

name: Chilean Kuchen de Manzana

ingredients.ingredient: Apples, raw, gala, with skin (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Cream the butter and sugar, then work in the flour to form a soft crust; press into a small baking dish.
Arrange the sliced apple over the top.
Bake at 350F for 30-35 minutes until the crust is golden and the apples are tender.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Apples, raw, gala, with skin (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Cream the butter and sugar until light and fluffy, then beat in the egg.
Sift together the flour, baking powder, and cinnamon, and fold into the butter mixture along with the milk, alternating, until just combined into a soft batter.
Spread about two-thirds of the batter into a small buttered baking dish, then arrange the sliced apple over the top and dot with the remaining batter.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 30-35 minutes until golden on top and a toothpick comes out clean.
Let cool slightly before slicing - this is meant to be a rustic, homey cake, not overly neat.

name: Chilean Pebre
steps: Combine the tomatoes, onion, cilantro, and jalapeno in a bowl.
Season with salt, a splash of oil, and lime juice to taste.
Let sit 10 minutes before serving to let the flavors meld.
steps_detailed: Finely dice the tomatoes if not already, and combine with the onion, garlic, cilantro, and jalapeno in a bowl.
Stir in the lime juice and oil, and season generously with salt.
Let it sit at room temperature for at least 10-15 minutes before serving so the onion softens and the flavors come together.
Serve alongside bread, empanadas, or grilled meats.

name: Chilean Chancho en Piedra
steps: Mash the tomatoes with the garlic and jalapeno until mostly smooth but still a bit chunky.
Stir in the oil and season with salt.
Serve at room temperature with bread.
steps_detailed: Traditionally mashed in a stone mortar, but a bowl and fork work fine: mash the tomatoes with the garlic and jalapeno until you have a rustic, chunky paste.
Stir in the oil and cilantro, and season well with salt.
Let sit 10 minutes for the garlic to mellow slightly, then serve at room temperature with crusty bread.
